sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 46-year-old male, presented to the office complaining of back pain that started 2 hours ago after raking leaves in his yard.
He describes the pain as a tightening sensation in his back and pins and needles in his right foot.
He reports that the pain is worse when standing up and improved with a hot shower.
He denies taking any other treatments for the pain, but mentions that he took 2 Advil and applied ice.
He reports a similar episode 3 years ago that resolved on its own.
Past medical history: The patient reports that he normally does not engage in physical activity, but his partner reports that he has been doing a lot of yard work lately.
The patient denies any other medical conditions or allergies.
Review of systems: The patient reports tingling sensation in his right leg.
Current medications: None mentioned.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient had some pain on flexion, pain to palpation around the L5 level, and normal dorsiflexion.
Test Results: X-ray of the low back was normal.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely sprained his low back.
Problem list: Lower back sprain; suspected radiculopathy; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5 mg orally once daily for pain management.
I also prescribed physical therapy to help with stretches and exercises to improve mobility and alleviate pain.
I instructed the patient to rest the affected area and follow up with me in 2 weeks if the pain does not improve.
If the pain persists, we will consider further testing, including an MRI.
Follow-up: The patient is to follow up with me in 2 weeks to reassess the pain and determine if further testing is necessary.
